content.problemStatement:
Write a C program that takes an integer representing the day of the week (1 to 7) as input and prints the corresponding day's name. 

If the given integer is any number other than 1 to 7 - then you need to output 'Invalid day number'.

Use a switch statement to implement this.

Check the sample input / output below to for further clarity.
constraints: Day integer fits in a 32-bit signed integer (may be negative or larger than 7)
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer representing the day of the week (1 for Monday … 7 for Sunday).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the corresponding day name (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday) on its own line. If the integer is not between 1 and 7 output "Invalid day number".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases are processed. 2 maps to Tuesday, 7 maps to Sunday, and 10 is outside 1‑7 so the program prints "Invalid day number".
A single test case with day 1 outputs Monday.
Day 5 maps to Friday; 0 is invalid, so the corresponding message is printed.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Sunday
Invalid day number
Invalid day number
Wednesday
Saturday

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Invalid day number
Invalid day number
Invalid day number
Invalid day number

hidden.label: tricky edge
hidden.expectedOutput:
Sunday
Saturday
Friday
Thursday
Wednesday
Tuesday

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Monday
Tuesday
Tuesday
Invalid day number
Sunday
Monday
Invalid day number
Friday

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the integer and use a switch statement (or equivalent if‑else chain) to map values 1‑7 to their weekday names. Any other value falls to the default case and prints "Invalid day number". This runs in O(T) time with O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
